title: Acne Scarring: Can Dermal Fillers Help？ | Dr Tim's Tips

excerpt: So here's a common question do dermal fillers help with acne scarring well the simple answer is yes but the more complex answer is it depends each scar is unique and really what we're trying to figure out is um is there something you can do by adding volume that will make the sca…

segments.text: so here's a common question do dermal fillers help with acne scarring well the simple answer is yes but the more complex answer is it depends each scar is unique and really what we're trying to figure out is um is there something you can do by adding volume that will make the scar less visible and broadly speaking there are kind of two types of scars that are hypertrophic and atrophic scars so some are protruding from the skin surface and some are recessed into the skin surface and then there are just kind of complex networks of collagen that you can see a texture on top of the skin and ultimately you've got to make a decision on every single scar that you see individually and the answer wouldn't be yes or no it's definitely it depends and if you have for example a nice big scar where there's an indentation into the surface of the skin and you place a little bit of product just underneath that can raise the surface of the skin just where it needs

to be and then that means the light doesn't catch it so really what we're talking about is the way the light interacts with the skin and so if you can think of a way of making the light in the lights interaction less sharp so either less of a deep line or shadow or just by harmonizing the curvature of the skin raising the surface of the skin slightly these are all ways that you basically allow the light to more harmoniously cast itself from top of the skin so you can even do it I have actually successfully treated a northern - hypertrophic scar but it was a protrusion a mole that basically protrudes out of the skin and there was a line that was catching the light so just by filling just next to it made it much easier to for it to harmonize into the skin and also less likely to kick so you collect makeup in the area so similar principles apply with any scar and you must be careful though certain scars you can't add what that much volume to the skin can be quite thick and some sometimes you'll get

no difference at all the other thing to consider is that where the scar meets where the two parts of the skin have healed you often get a white line over that filler will not change a white line it will stay a different color and you should advise your patients it doesn't affect the colored part of the line it only affects the way that the light interacts with the scar so you were moving shadows and harmonizing and but not actually removing the scar so the helps [Music] you

transcript: so here's a common question do dermal fillers help with acne scarring well the simple answer is yes but the more complex answer is it depends each scar is unique and really what we're trying to figure out is um is there something you can do by adding volume that will make the scar less visible and broadly speaking there are kind of two types of scars that are hypertrophic and atrophic scars so some are protruding from the skin surface and some are recessed into the skin surface and then there are just kind of complex networks of collagen that you can see a texture on top of the skin and ultimately you've got to make a decision on every single scar that you see individually and the answer wouldn't be yes or no it's definitely it depends and if you have for example a nice big scar where there's an indentation into the surface of the skin and you place a little bit of product just underneath that can raise the surface of the skin just where it needs to be and then that means the light doesn't catch it so really what we're talking about is the way the light interacts with the skin and so if you can think of a way of making the light in the lights interaction less sharp so either less of a deep line or shadow or just by harmonizing the curvature of the skin raising the surface of the skin slightly these are all ways that you basically allow the light to more harmoniously cast itself from top of the skin so you can even do it I have actually successfully treated a northern - hypertrophic scar but it was a protrusion a mole that basically protrudes out of the skin and there was a line that was catching the light so just by filling just next to it made it much easier to for it to harmonize into the skin and also less likely to kick so you collect makeup in the area so similar principles apply with any scar and you must be careful though certain scars you can't add what that much volume to the skin can be quite thick and some sometimes you'll get no difference at all the other thing to consider is that where the scar meets where the two parts of the skin have healed you often get a white line over that filler will not change a white line it will stay a different color and you should advise your patients it doesn't affect the colored part of the line it only affects the way that the light interacts with the scar so you were moving shadows and harmonizing and but not actually removing the scar so the helps [Music] you
